---
title: "Data Science and Digital Skills Programme"
subtitle: "Building core competencies for modern, reproducible research"

format:
  html:
    theme: 
        - default
        - ./uon.scss
    toc: false      
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: full   
    sidebar: false 

about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: jolla
  image: assets/Logo_white_500.png
  links:
    - icon: twitter
      text: Twitter
      href: https://twitter.com
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com
---

:::{#hero-heading}
The **Data Science & Digital Skills Programme (DS&DSP)** is a one-year, in-person training initiative that equips researchers with the essential digital competencies for modern, reproducible, and data-intensive research. 
:::

---

# Programme Overview

::::: grid
::: g-col-3
![Training Tiers](assets/Training_Teirs.jpg){width="100%"}
:::
::: g-col-9
In an environment where computational literacy and responsible data handling are essential, DS&DSP provides a structured and progressive training pathway aligned with open science and research integrity goals.
:::
:::::

---

# Training Tiers

## Foundation 

### Essential Digital Skills

::::: grid
::: g-col-7
**Essential Digital Skills (EDS)** is the foundation tier, delivered through four full-day, in-person sessions. It includes 8 themed modules covering:

- Institutional tools and infrastructure
- Governance and data policy
- FAIR data principles
- Core tools like Git, Quarto, R, and Python

Modules use short lectures, scenario-based tasks, and collaborative activities with a research-focused framing.
:::
::: g-col-5
![EDS](assets/Compulsory_Training.jpg){width="100%"}
:::
:::::

---

## Intermediate

### Theoretical Pathway

::::: grid
::: g-col-5
![Theoretical Pathway](assets/Theoretical_Pathway.jpg){width="100%"}
:::
::: g-col-7
Focuses on conceptual and methodological foundations for data-intensive research across disciplines.
:::
:::::

---

### Applied Pathway (Python)

::::: grid
::: g-col-5
![Applied Pathway – Python](assets/Applied_Pathway_Python.jpg){width="100%"}
:::
::: g-col-7
Focuses on hands-on data wrangling, analysis, and automation using Python, with structured lesson modules and exercises.
:::
:::::

---

### Applied Pathway (R)

::::: grid
::: g-col-5
![Applied Pathway – R](assets/Applied_Pathway_R.jpg){width="100%"}
:::
::: g-col-7
Targets R users interested in reproducible workflows and data visualisation in a modern applied context.
:::
:::::

## Standalone Modules

In addtion to the core course we are also developing targeted training in domain specfic areas including bioinformatics and HPC. 

![Standalone Modules](assets/Standalone_modules.jpg){width="100%"}

---

# Structure and Delivery

:::::: grid
::: g-col-6
### Structure

The DS&DS Programme spans three levels:

- **Foundation**: *Essential Digital Skills* (Year 1, mandatory)
- **Intermediate**: *Methods for Data Science* (Year 2+, optional)
- **Advanced**: *Domain-Specific Training* (Year 2–3+, tailored)

Progression ensures increased confidence and research capability at each stage.
:::
::: g-col-6
### Delivery

- Delivered primarily in-person
- Includes workshops, exercises, and cohort-based activities
- Initial delivery targeted to BBSRC DTP students (2025)
- All materials developed for open reuse and dissemination
:::
::::::

---

# Learning Outcomes

Participants will:

- Apply FAIR principles to manage research data
- Use Git, Quarto, and Jupyter for reproducible workflows
- Analyse data using discipline-relevant tools
- Document and share research outputs openly
- Navigate data governance and ethics

All course materials are open-source and available via GitHub.

---

# Access Materials

::: panel-tabset
### Essential Digital Skills
👉 [Explore the EDS Programme](essential_digital_skills/index.html)
:::
  
---

# Participant Info

- Open to BBSRC doctoral researchers at the University of Nottingham
- Suitable for those with limited or no coding experience
- Delivered in a discipline-agnostic, inclusive format
- Encourages collaboration and peer learning

---

# Contact

**Programme Lead**: *Dr. Thomas Giles*  
📧 **tom.giles@nottingham.ac.uk**

For registration or accessibility queries, please contact the DS&DS team.

---
